text:
Once upon a time in a sleepy little town called Willowbrook, lived a boy named Freddy. Freddy was known for making mischief, and he was always up to something crazy. One autumn morning, as the leaves danced in the vibrant hues of decay, Freddy hatched a scheme that was as absurd as it was brilliant.

He set his sights on Ms. Arlene Weaver's vegetable patch. Her tomatoes were the envy of all the neighbors, and she was never seen without a few slicing into a nutritious slice of paradise. Freddy, however, planned something extraordinary. He created a costume of giant green ears and bulging eyes, fashioned out of recycled material from his mom's quilts—part rabbit, part monster.

With his new “monster ears,” Freddy tiptoed through the garden at dusk. As he approached the ripe tomatoes, holding a giant red apple, he made such a comical noise—squeak squeak—every leaf rustled, and his eyes sparkled with mischief.
